page: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows balanced performance across various workloads. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a Passmark CPU score of 48594.14, which is categorized as strong, alongside a strong CPU encryption test score of 43612.59. In memory-bound operations, the instance achieves a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 2503.74 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 68.54 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the server demonstrates strong image processing capabilities with a Geekbench score of 15061.0, and handles small-scale LLM inference efficiently, reaching 2290.82 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model. However, performance drops to a weak rating of 2.75 tokens/sec for text generation on a large 70B model, highlighting a tradeoff when running massive parameter models without GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6i.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for workloads that require steady-state compute and memory without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale text or image processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Nitro hypervisor, this server provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in CPU encryption and image processing, alongside solid multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations. It handles small-scale language model inference efficiently, though text generation on large 70B models is a notable bottleneck. With a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps, the m6i.12xlarge represents a resource-dense, cost-efficient solution for steady-state workloads. It is highly suited for applications such as static web serving, database management, software building, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering balanced compute and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor under the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed memory for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
Intel Xeon 8375C processor with a 3.5 GHz clock speed
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong performance in CPU encryption and image processing benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and software compilation
